Request Title: Number of bearings in WSP pumps
Request Description: Client (KOC) are asking about standard number of bearings in our pumps, and if it is possible to increase such bearings along the pump assemble to increase/ enhance stability. as they do have some confirmed cases where they have lost mechanical sealing in the seal section due to vibrations in the pump section which can be avoided by increasing such bearings.

	Injectivity test: to check perforation: 

•	 R/U pump on casing since the whole job will be pumping through the annulus
•	 The maximum pumping pressure = 1000 psi.
•	Start pumping water :
a)	if the pressure increases that means we have a blockage in the perforations
b)	if the pressure start to decrease that’s means the perforations are fine and not blocked

	Stimulation by BH : if case (b) is accrue during the injectivity , we will proceed with the stimulation 

3 stages of stimulation will be pumped (Each stage = 13 ft)  as follows the sequence will be ( Break down Acid , emulsified acid , acid spacer , diverter , Break down Acid , emulsified acid , acid spacer , diverter….. etc.):
•	45 bbl of Pre – Flush ( water + 10% mutual solvent) 
•	30 gal/ft break down acid 15% HCl 
•	75 gal/ft emulsified Acid 
•	2 bbl Acid spacer 
•	25 gal/ft diverter ( excluding the last stage ) 
•	Then displace the acid by water (2* wellbore volume ) = 90 bbl 

During the job another pump will be rigged up thru the casing & will pump 40 bbl of diesel @min rate to push the treatment into the formation & prevent the acid to go upward toward the ESP cable (MAX.WHP: 1000 psi )

The above program will be applied for one of our wells, and client is asking if our equipment can handle the above quantities without failure.

Request Title: TEST VOLTAGES FOR HIPOT, P.I. AND SURGE TESTING
Request Description:
Review the VDC to be applied during motor testing procedure.  
QAITI011 Rev.6:  Procedure indicates to apply 11000 VDC for all new motors and 9000 VDC for all repaired motors.
QAITI011 Rev.6:   Procedure indicates to apply the following formula:
New Motors : (2xMotor Nameplate Voltage+1000)x1.7
Repaired Motors: (2xMotor Nameplate Voltage+1000)x1.2
For instance, for the motor 120 HP/1625V/46 A to be repaired:
•	QAITI011 Rev.6 :  Test Voltage to apply must be 9000 VDC
•	QAITI011 Rev.5 :  Test Voltage to apply must be 5100 VDC
•	The difference is 3900 VDC or 76,4% or VDC more , this could cause damage.

Request Title: MBD-1706 Design with sand history  problem
Request Description:
Eni requested a design for an onshore well with sand problems history. It was run twice by Borets with the following run lives: 
1-	First installation : ~800 days 
2-	Second installation : 276 days 

In both cases the well was plugged with sand , however, no treatment was done in the last run. Actually our crew did the pullout after the last failure in 2015 and Eni decided to abandon the well since there was no treatment programmed for the well and no sand screens available. 

 This time the WO programme is as follows: 

•	Control well as per DIFO procedures
•	POOH killing string.
•	Clean well down to top of lower completion.
•	CT wash sand inside lower completion and front of perforation interval 
•	Perform Acid stimulation with CT
•	RIH with Sand control system (Stand-Alone screen as done in MBD-403)
•	RIH with ESP

I requested some more info like  sand samples etc but not available. I was only provided with the datasheet of the sand screen (attached). 

My concern is that with this incentive contract , it looks like there is a risk of not plugging during the warranty period (730 days). There is no solid data at hand to evaluate that risk , Eni believes that the proposed WO programme and sand screen can help prolong the run life. 

I need your help and input on this.

Customer: Saudi Aramco - Abu Saafa Oil Well MMF (ABSF)
Request Description:
we have a case in ABSF field with ESP with low flow rate, below summary of the case:
* pump: WG4000/79 stg.
* Motor: 157 HP/ 3043V / 30.7A.

on 24 Aug, we noticed there were an issue with amps of the motor as the deadhead amps were higher than the running amps. the issue was discussed with Eng. Rami and suspect an issue with the thrust washer causing a metal to metal friction while deadheading.

after that the well was running with stable behavior and inside the pump curve, then Aramco reported low flow from this well.( well has history of choke issue ).  visited the well and found the performance stable.

after that the well tripped on high motor temperature, did analysis to the trip and Pd was lost during the troubleshooting, increased frequency to have more cooling for the motor

agreed with Aramco on the below plan:
-	As there is flowing issue, we need to eliminate the surface problems by performing the below:
o	Exercise the SSSV. 
o	Fix the choke issue.
o	Make sure the MPFM is working OK by testing it from another well.
o	Confirm all surface gauges are calibrated specially US and DS.
-	After confirming the surface all OK, start the well on deadhead then open the choke slowly and confirm all parameters.
-	Monitor the TCA.
-	Monitor WHP, intake pressure, motor temperature and surface rate from MPFM.
 
the team visited the well with AFS to do the action plan, AFS check all valves, TCA pressure was stable and we have high WHP and also did exercise the SSSV.
increased the frequency to 65 then to 70 and notice a little bit decrease on intake pressure and motor temperature with little increase in wellhead pressure.

note: full details of the case is sent by email.

SFNY-167 is not performing (No flow-Avg amps- Low WHP)

Motor: 157 HP/38.9A/2,433V
Pump: WE5500/48 Stg

This well was running ok until tripped due to over volt and under current on the same time, when we changed the rotation or close the choke no build up WHP and when open the choke no flow on surface. 
we ran the ESP at different Hz and different choke settings including closed choke and we got no response from ESP. 
Wireline confirmed no issue with SSSV. 
all details were sent by email.

Request Title: ESP Running Procedures and Design/Installation Limitations
Request Description:
To support future design, installation, and operational activities, we would appreciate receiving your latest ESP running procedure and installation guidelines.

 

Having documented vendor requirements available will help ensure that operational decisions are consistent and supported by established guidelines.

 

Please provide your latest ESP running procedure and installation guidelines, including detailed requirements, limitations, recommendations, and restrictions related to the following:

 

Maximum allowable DLS at PSD, along with the maximum allowable inclination.
Maximum allowable DLS and inclination during ESP RIH, including criteria for performing stress analysis and any limitations related to ESP maximum OD versus casing drift, including the impact of equipment length, liner existence, and liner top crossing on installation feasibility.
Maximum allowable DLS across liner tops, if applicable.
Minimum "ESP assembly versus casing drift" clearance requirements.
Free gas handling limitations and maximum allowable free gas at pump intake, including gas handler requirements.
Minimum required pump intake pressure and any operating limitations related to gas locking, up-thrust, down-thrust, or unstable pump operation.
In case of equipment becoming stuck during RIH, the maximum allowable slack-off and pick-up loads, as well as any rotation restrictions and operational limitations.
Running speed recommendations (normal running, through restrictions, across liner tops, etc.).
ESP assembly rotation restrictions and limitations.
Recommended actions prior to deciding to POOH in case of equipment hold-up, restrictions, or excessive drag, including any circulation requirements or recommendations.
Conditions requiring mandatory POOH, if applicable.
Cable protector / clamp types for each casing drift size, including dimensions, clearance requirements, and any associated installation limitations.
Any other operational limitations or installation requirements applicable to
